title: These 2 Business Services Stocks Could Beat Earnings: Why They Should Be on Your Radar
text: Investors looking for ways to find stocks that are set to beat quarterly earnings estimates should check out the Zacks Earnings ESP.

title: 3 Stocks to Take Advantage of a Rising Outsourcing Industry
text: The Zacks Outsourcing industry is expected to prosper with the rising importance of business and IT outsourcing. ADP, PAYX and BR are well poised to gain from the prevailing trends.

title: BBSI vs. PAYX: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
text: Investors looking for stocks in the Outsourcing sector might want to consider either Barrett Business Services (BBSI) or Paychex (PAYX). But which of these two stocks is more attractive to value investors?

title: Here's Why You Should Retain ADP Stock in Your Portfolio
text: ADP's three-tier business strategy is helping it to maintain and grow its strong position as a HCM technology and services provider.

title: Paychex Named a Leader in Payroll Services by NelsonHall
text: ROCHESTER, N.Y.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--NelsonHall, a global analyst and research firm, has once again identified Paychex, Inc., a leading provider of integrated human capital management software solutions for human resources, employee benefits, insurance services, and payroll, as a “Leader” in its 2023 NelsonHall Vendor Evaluation and Assessment Tool (NEAT) report for payroll service providers. This is the seventh consecutive time Paychex has been positioned in the upper right quadrant of the NEAT g.

title: 3 payroll companies get clocked, but 1 looks cheap
text: Payroll companies alleviate the complexities of processing payrolls. They withhold taxes, apply payments to various government agencies, track employee hours, track benefits, comply with regulations, and calculate and process employee paychecks.

title: BBSI or PAYX: Which Is the Better Value Stock Right Now?
text: Investors with an interest in Outsourcing stocks have likely encountered both Barrett Business Services (BBSI) and Paychex (PAYX). But which of these two stocks presents investors with the better value opportunity right now?

title: 4 Factor Dividend Growth Portfolio: Year One Is On The Books
text: The 4-factor dividend growth portfolio is a strategy that leverages the stock selection process of Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF, with a few minor twists. The portfolio fell by 3.07% in October, underperforming the S&P 500 by 0.97%. Year-to-date, the portfolio is up 3.61%.
